commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The protected maintainer label plus mock-only proof make this a human review and validation item rather than an auto-close or repair-lane candidate.; Cleared: The diff touches gateway scheduling, focused tests, and changelog only; it does not add dependencies, CI/workflow execution, secrets handling, install scripts, or package metadata changes. (sha=4adc0b9eb78e90b392659f42f1a86fe9665d5390)
commands.review_summary:
The PR coalesces gateway provider auth-state rewarms after auth-profile failures, adds auth warm timing/event-loop delay logs, updates focused gateway tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level scheduler behavior: current main starts a warm on every auth-profile failure hook, and the branch tests the coalesced delayed path. No high-confidence live Telegram/provider-auth failure reproduction is included.
commands.review_followup: The protected `maintainer` label plus mock-only proof make this a human review and validation item rather than an auto-close or repair-lane candidate.

target.title: fix(gateway): coalesce provider auth rewarms
target.body:
## Summary

- Coalesce provider auth-state rewarms triggered by auth-profile failures so a burst of provider failures schedules one delayed rewarm instead of starting overlapping full auth sweeps.
- Keep immediate auth-state invalidation, but cap rewarm work to one scheduled/in-flight job with one pending follow-up if another failure arrives mid-run.
- Log provider auth warm/rewarm elapsed time plus event-loop max delay so future Telegram/channel latency reports can be measured from gateway logs.
- Add a changelog entry because this affects user-visible channel responsiveness.

## Observation / Regression

Discord report on 2026-05-22: latest main made Telegram very slow; after sending a message, reaction took 1-2 minutes. The reporter also saw a note about provider auth pre-warm exhausting the event loop.

History points at the provider auth warm path:

- 95343affbb expanded provider auth warm to all configured agents and all providers.
- 55cfe00a3a made auth-profile failure/invalidation immediately schedule provider auth rewarm.
- 69255f8f32 deferred startup prewarm after readiness, but preserved the immediate failure-triggered rewarm path.
- v2026.5.21-beta.1 and origin/release/2026.5.21 contain all of the above, so that beta is in the at-risk state.

Before this patch, each auth-profile failure cleared the prepared state and immediately launched warmCurrentProviderAuthState. A burst of failures during a bad provider/model turn could stack overlapping all-agent/all-provider auth sweeps behind channel replies.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: provider auth-profile failure bursts no longer spawn overlapping provider auth-state rewarms, and provider auth warm/rewarm logs include event-loop delay.
Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout on Node 26 through the repo test wrapper.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=60000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-cache-provider-auth-rewarm-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts
- pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
- /Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
Evidence after fix: focused gateway post-attach test file passed with 41 tests; formatter and diff whitespace checks passed; autoreview returned no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: auth-profile failure hook clears current provider auth state immediately, then one delayed rewarm fires after 1s; repeated hooks before the timer fires coalesce and use the latest config.
What was not tested: a live Telegram bot roundtrip with real provider auth failures; the new eventLoopMax log is included so that can be measured on a live gateway.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Policy: add agent workspace conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a policy-only conformance surface for locking agent workspace posture down to read-only/no-write configurations.

- records agent/default sandbox `workspaceAccess` and tool deny coverage as policy evidence
- adds `agents.workspace.allowedAccess` and `agents.workspace.denyTools` checks
- documents the supported read-only policy shape, evidence payload, and attestation-hash impact

This is stacked after #81981 and intentionally stays at config/policy conformance level; it does not add runtime enforcement.

## Policy syntax

```jsonc
{
  "agents": {
    "workspace": {
      "allowedAccess": ["none", "ro"],
      "denyTools": ["exec", "process", "write", "edit", "apply_patch"],
    },
  },
}
```

Enabling or upgrading these rules adds `agentWorkspace` evidence to the workspace and attestation hashes. Operators should review the new evidence and refresh accepted attestation hashes after enabling these rules.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts --run --reporter=dot` (113 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts --run --reporter=dot` (12 passed)
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-policy-agent-workspace-rebased.tsbuildinfo`
- `env PATH="/tmp/npm-11-6-2-bin:$PATH" node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --all --check` (matches CI npm 11.6.2)
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Policy conformance can require agent sandbox workspace access to stay within an allowlist and require explicit deny coverage for shell/process/file-mutation tools.
Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-policy-agent-workspace` on Ubuntu-24.04.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/policy-proof-85096/openclaw.json timeout 180 node --import tsx -e "import('./src/cli/run-main.ts').then(m=>m.runCli(['node','openclaw','policy','check','--json','--severity-min','error']))"`
Evidence after fix: Malformed policy shape produced `policy/policy-jsonc-invalid`; nonconforming config produced `policy/agents-workspace-access-denied` and `policy/agents-tool-not-denied`; accepted read-only posture produced `ok: true` with zero findings.
Observed result after fix: The policy check emitted `agentWorkspace` evidence and 31 policy checks, including the new agent workspace checks.
What was not tested: No runtime sandbox/tool enforcement was tested because this PR only adds config-level policy conformance.

### Redacted CLI evidence

Malformed policy:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/policy-jsonc-invalid",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "policy.jsonc agents.workspace.allowedAccess must be an array.",
      "target": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/allowedAccess"
    }
  ]
}
```

Nonconforming config:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/agents-workspace-access-denied",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "agents.defaults sandbox workspaceAccess 'rw' is not allowed by policy.",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/sandbox/workspaceAccess",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/allowedAccess"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/agents-tool-not-denied",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "agents.defaults does not deny required tool 'apply_patch'.",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/tools/deny",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/denyTools"
    }
  ]
}
```

Accepted read-only posture:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [],
  "agentWorkspace": [
    {
      "id": "agents-defaults-workspace-access",
      "kind": "workspaceAccess",
      "source": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/sandbox/workspaceAccess",
      "scope": "defaults",
      "value": "ro",
      "explicit": true
    },
    {
      "id": "agents-defaults-tool-apply_patch",
      "kind": "toolDeny",
      "source": "oc://openclaw.config/tools/deny",
      "scope": "defaults",
      "tool": "apply_patch",
      "denied": true,
      "explicit": true
    }
  ]
}
```
target.author: giodl73-repo

- `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Policy conformance can require agent sandbox workspace access to stay within an allowlist and require explicit deny coverage for shell/process/file-mutation tools.
Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-policy-agent-workspace` on Ubuntu-24.04.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/policy-proof-85096/openclaw.json timeout 180 node --import tsx -e "import('./src/cli/run-main.ts').then(m=>m.runCli(['node','openclaw','policy','check','--json','--severity-min','error']))"`
Evidence after fix: Malformed policy shape produced `policy/policy-jsonc-invalid`; nonconforming config produced `policy/agents-workspace-access-denied` and `policy/agents-tool-not-denied`; accepted read-only posture produced `ok: true` with zero findings.
Observed result after fix: The policy check emitted `agentWorkspace` evidence and 31 policy checks, including the new agent workspace checks.
What was not tested: No runtime sandbox/tool enforcement was tested because this PR only adds config-level policy conformance.

### Redacted CLI evidence

Malformed policy:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/policy-jsonc-invalid",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "policy.jsonc agents.workspace.allowedAccess must be an array.",
      "target": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/allowedAccess"
    }
  ]
}
```

Nonconforming config:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/agents-workspace-access-denied",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "agents.defaults sandbox workspaceAccess 'rw' is not allowed by policy.",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/sandbox/workspaceAccess",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/allowedAccess"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/agents-tool-not-denied",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "agents.defaults does not deny required tool 'apply_patch'.",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/tools/deny",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/denyTools"
    }
  ]
}
```

Accepted read-only posture:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [],
  "agentWorkspace": [
    {
      "id": "agents-defaults-workspace-access",
      "kind": "workspaceAccess",
      "source": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/sandbox/workspaceAccess",
      "scope": "defaults",
      "value": "ro",
      "explicit": true
    },
    {
      "id": "agents-defaults-tool-apply_patch",
      "kind": "toolDeny",
      "source": "oc://openclaw.config/tools/deny",
      "scope": "defaults",
      "tool": "apply_patch",
      "denied": true,
      "explicit": true
    }
  ]
}
```
